summary: Reporter sees flickering when continuously drawing an SKBitmap via a custom NativeAOT Android JNI bridge using GLSurfaceView + GRContext (OpenGL backend) on SkiaSharp 3.118.0-preview.2.

bugSignals.errorMessage: Visual flickering when drawing SKBitmap continuously

stepsToReproduce: Create a NativeAOT Android app with GLSurfaceView.Renderer
Create GRContext, GRBackendRenderTarget, SKSurface backed by the GL framebuffer
Each frame: erase a reused SKBitmap with a random color and call canvas.DrawBitmap()
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: SkiaSharp 3.118.0-preview.2.3, dotnet 9, Android Studio 2024, Visual Studio 2022 on Windows

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Uses a preview version; the OpenGL rendering path has not changed significantly since.

analysis.summary: Flickering likely caused by a combination of: (1) missing canvas.Flush() before context.Flush(), (2) using synchronous Flush(true,true) which can cause frame-timing issues with GLSurfaceView's double-buffer swap, and (3) the fixed-size SKBitmap (950x1000) drawn at origin may not cover the entire render target leaving partial undefined content.
analysis.rationale: The code shows correct structure (GRContext, GRBackendRenderTarget, SKSurface) but the flush sequence appears incomplete — canvas.Flush() should be called before context.Flush() to ensure all Skia commands are submitted. GLSurfaceView manages its own buffer swap after onDrawFrame returns, and calling context.Flush(synchronous:true) can cause CPU-GPU synchronization stalls that interact badly with this swap. The bitmap size (950x1000) is hard-coded and may not match the actual render target dimensions, leaving uncleared regions.

codeInvestigation.finding: GRContext.Flush(bool submit, bool synchronous) maps to gr_direct_context_flush_and_submit. Calling Flush(true, true) performs a synchronous CPU-GPU sync on every frame, which can cause stalls when combined with GLSurfaceView's own buffer swap timing.
SKCanvas.DrawBitmap(SKBitmap, float x, float y) exists and is public. There is no automatic flush after DrawBitmap; the caller is responsible for flushing the canvas before flushing the context.

keySignals.text: if i draw a single bitmap,it is work ok,However, if keep drawing, the problem of flickering will occur
keySignals.interpretation: Flickering only appears during continuous rendering — consistent with a double-buffering / flush-ordering issue rather than a one-shot rendering bug.
Synchronous flush on every frame can cause CPU stalls and timing skew relative to GLSurfaceView's internal buffer swap. canvas.Flush() is not called before context.Flush().
Fixed-size bitmap (950x1000) drawn at (0,0) may not cover the full render target; uncleared pixels outside this region could contribute to flickering.

workarounds: Call canvas.Flush() before context.Flush() to ensure all Skia draw commands are submitted to the GPU command buffer.
Use context.Flush(true, false) (async flush) instead of context.Flush(true, true) to avoid synchronous CPU-GPU stalls on every frame.
Size the SKBitmap to match the actual render target dimensions (or use canvas.Clear(SKColors.Black) before drawing to avoid partial undefined regions).
Consider converting the SKBitmap to SKImage before each draw using SKImage.FromBitmap() — SKImage is GPU-cached and avoids repeated CPU-to-GPU uploads per frame.
nextQuestions: Does the flickering disappear when using context.Flush(true, false) (asynchronous flush)?
Does the flickering persist if SKColors.Black replaces SKColors.Transparent in canvas.Clear()?
What are the actual screen dimensions vs the bitmap size (950x1000)?
Is the NativeAOT aspect relevant, or does this also happen with standard JVM-managed .NET Android?

resolution.hypothesis: The missing canvas.Flush() and/or the synchronous context flush are causing frame-timing issues with GLSurfaceView's double-buffer swap, causing some frames to be partially or incompletely rendered.

proposals.title: Fix flush sequence and switch to async flush
proposals.description: Add canvas.Flush() before context.Flush(), and use async flush to avoid stalling the GL thread.

proposals.title: Use SKImage instead of SKBitmap for GPU-side rendering
proposals.description: Convert the bitmap to SKImage once per update to allow GPU-side caching and avoid repeated CPU→GPU uploads on every draw frame.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Fix flush sequence and switch to async flush
resolution.recommendedReason: Minimal change, targets the most likely root cause (flush ordering and synchronous stall), and matches the standard SkiaSharp GL rendering pattern.

actionability.reason: Code is provided and the bug seems real; the NativeAOT + custom JNI bridge makes this hard to reproduce without the reporter's setup. Needs investigation on Android with NativeAOT enabled.

actions.description: Apply bug, core API, Android, OpenGL labels
Ask reporter to try flush sequence fix and provide feedback
